Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search the loan pipeline using mortgage_los_search_loans or mortgage_los_get_pipeline to identify loans that are stuck or stagnant. The pipeline contains multiple clear_to_close loans that are past their closing dates with expired rate locks, plus additional processing-status loans with lock issues. The agent should discover several stuck files: the most critical include LN-2026-00619 (clear_to_close, lock expired 3/21, closing date 3/17 passed by 40 days), LN-2026-00601, LN-2026-00554, LN-2026-00545, LN-2026-00552, and LN-2026-00621 (all CTC with expired or expiring locks and past or imminent closing dates). Agents may also discover LN-2026-00616 (processing, lock expired 3/31, suspended), LN-2026-00610 (processing, lock expired 3/30), and LN-2026-00622 (processing, Camille flagged as preventable expiration) via Slack/CRM context. The agent should identify the assigned loan officers and processors for each file to determine who is responsible.
OE 2: Pull conditions on the flagged loans using mortgage_los_get_conditions (loan_id for each affected file) to understand what is outstanding or was improperly cleared. On the QC-flagged files (LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00224), the agent should find conditions marked as cleared in the LOS without supporting documentation actually present in the file. Raymond's QC report and CRM engagement notes identify Tyler Washington as the person who cleared them.
OE 3: Search emails using search_emails (query: "pipeline" or "lock" or "Sofia" or "Raymond" or "QC" or "conditions" or similar) to find internal discussions about the delays and QC concerns. Key discoveries include Raymond Chen's email to Grace reporting his post-close spot check results on three exception loans (LN-2026-00603 for Heather Sullivan, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00224) where Tyler Washington cleared conditions without supporting docs in the file. The agent should also find Grace's email to Sofia asking for an explanation on LN-2026-00619, Grace's email to Tyler about processing quality concerns, and Grace's forwarding of Raymond's findings to Denise for a compliance read on the closed-file exposure.
OE 4: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "lock" or "expir" or "pipeline" or "Sofia" or "processing" or similar) or conversations_history on #loan-processing (C002) and #rate-watch (C005) to find internal discussions about the delays and lock expirations. The agent should discover Camille Foster flagging LN-2026-00619 lock expiration in #rate-watch (C005), Camille flagging LN-2026-00610 and LN-2026-00616 lock/suspension issues in #loan-processing (C002), Grace's message requesting a Sofia pipeline review and scheduling time to walk through workload and the LN-2026-00616 failure, and Camille's note about 2 preventable expirations on Carlos Rivera's files (LN-2026-00622 and LN-2026-00211) in #rate-watch (C005).
OE 5: Search CRM engagement notes using crm_list_engagements to find logged interactions about the pipeline breakdown. Key discoveries include Sofia's panicked call with Carlos where she described being buried under 34 active files and revealed the missed HOI binder condition on LN-2026-00616 (Destiny Pham) that caused the file to suspend and the lock to expire, Camille's three-way call with Carlos and Sofia about pricing a lock extension for Destiny's file, and Grace's internal pipeline review notes.
OE 6: Look up Elena Marchetti's and Denise Holloway's email addresses using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Elena" or "Marchetti") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Denise" or "Holloway") to confirm elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com and denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com for the outgoing emails.
OE 7: Add activity notes to the affected loan files using mortgage_los_add_activity or mortgage_los_update_loan for each loan that surfaced during investigation, including multiple stuck pipeline loans (at minimum LN-2026-00619 and several others with expired locks or past closing dates) and at least one of the QC-flagged loans (LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00224), with current status summaries reflecting what was found: backlog causes, lock expiration status, missed conditions, and QC concerns where applicable.
OE 8: Send an email using send_email (sender: grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, recipient: elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com) with a detailed breakdown of which loans are stuck, why they are stalled (Sofia's workload, missed conditions, lock expirations), who is responsible for each file, and what needs to happen to triage the backlog.
OE 9: Send an email using send_email (sender: grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, recipient: denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com) flagging the compliance exposure from Raymond's QC findings, specifically the three loans where Tyler Washington cleared conditions without supporting documentation, with emphasis on LN-2026-00603 (Heather Sullivan) which carries post-close liability since that file already went through closing.
OE 10: Post a message in #loan-processing (channel C002) using conversations_add_message with a status update letting the processing team know that pipeline delays and backlog issues have been identified, the stuck files are being triaged, and the team should expect follow-up on affected loans.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 1: Search the loan pipeline using mortgage_los_search_loans or mortgage_los_get_pipeline to identify loans that are stuck or stagnant. The pipeline contains multiple clear_to_close loans that are past their closing dates with expired rate locks, plus additional processing-status loans with lock issues. The agent should discover several stuck files: the most critical include LN-2026-00619 (clear_to_close, lock expired 3/21, closing date 3/17 passed by 40 days), LN-2026-00601, LN-2026-00554, LN-2026-00545, LN-2026-00552, and LN-2026-00621 (all CTC with expired or expiring locks and past or imminent closing dates). Agents may also discover LN-2026-00616 (processing, lock expired 3/31, suspended), LN-2026-00610 (processing, lock expired 3/30), and LN-2026-00622 (processing, Camille flagged as preventable expiration) via Slack/CRM context. The agent should identify the assigned loan officers and processors for each file to determine who is responsible.
OE 2: Pull conditions on the flagged loans using mortgage_los_get_conditions (loan_id for each affected file) to understand what is outstanding or was improperly cleared. On the QC-flagged files (LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00224), the agent should find conditions marked as cleared in the LOS without supporting documentation actually present in the file. Raymond's QC report and CRM engagement notes identify Tyler Washington as the person who cleared them.
OE 3: Search emails using search_emails (query: "pipeline" or "lock" or "Sofia" or "Raymond" or "QC" or "conditions" or similar) to find internal discussions about the delays and QC concerns. Key discoveries include Raymond Chen's email to Grace reporting his post-close spot check results on three exception loans (LN-2026-00603 for Heather Sullivan, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00224) where Tyler Washington cleared conditions without supporting docs in the file. The agent should also find Grace's email to Sofia asking for an explanation on LN-2026-00619, Grace's email to Tyler about processing quality concerns, and Grace's forwarding of Raymond's findings to Denise for a compliance read on the closed-file exposure.
OE 4: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "lock" or "expir" or "pipeline" or "Sofia" or "processing" or similar) or conversations_history on #loan-processing (C002) and #rate-watch (C005) to find internal discussions about the delays and lock expirations. The agent should discover Camille Foster flagging LN-2026-00619 lock expiration in #rate-watch (C005), Camille flagging LN-2026-00610 and LN-2026-00616 lock/suspension issues in #loan-processing (C002), Grace's message requesting a Sofia pipeline review and scheduling time to walk through workload and the LN-2026-00616 failure, and Camille's note about 2 preventable expirations on Carlos Rivera's files (LN-2026-00622 and LN-2026-00211) in #rate-watch (C005).
OE 5: Search CRM engagement notes using crm_list_engagements to find logged interactions about the pipeline breakdown. Key discoveries include Sofia's panicked call with Carlos where she described being buried under 34 active files and revealed the missed HOI binder condition on LN-2026-00616 (Destiny Pham) that caused the file to suspend and the lock to expire, Camille's three-way call with Carlos and Sofia about pricing a lock extension for Destiny's file, and Grace's internal pipeline review notes.
OE 6: Look up Elena Marchetti's and Denise Holloway's email addresses using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Elena" or "Marchetti") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Denise" or "Holloway") to confirm elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com and denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com for the outgoing emails.
OE 7: Add activity notes to the affected loan files using mortgage_los_add_activity or mortgage_los_update_loan for each loan that surfaced during investigation, including multiple stuck pipeline loans (at minimum LN-2026-00619 and several others with expired locks or past closing dates) and at least one of the QC-flagged loans (LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00224), with current status summaries reflecting what was found: backlog causes, lock expiration status, missed conditions, and QC concerns where applicable.
OE 8: Send an email using send_email (sender: grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, recipient: elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com) with a detailed breakdown of which loans are stuck, why they are stalled (Sofia's workload, missed conditions, lock expirations), who is responsible for each file, and what needs to happen to triage the backlog.
OE 9: Send an email using send_email (sender: grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, recipient: denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com) flagging the compliance exposure from Raymond's QC findings, specifically the three loans where Tyler Washington cleared conditions without supporting documentation, with emphasis on LN-2026-00603 (Heather Sullivan) which carries post-close liability since that file already went through closing.
OE 10: Post a message in #loan-processing (channel C002) using conversations_add_message with a status update letting the processing team know that pipeline delays and backlog issues have been identified, the stuck files are being triaged, and the team should expect follow-up on affected loans.

outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Elena clearly references multiple delay reasons including rate lock expirations, processor overload, missing document follow-up, compliance review friction, and files stuck in wrong statuses. These are detailed in a 'ROOT CAUSES' section.
✓Run #2The email to Elena explicitly identifies multiple delay reasons including rate lock expirations, loans assigned to terminated staff, processing backlog, and missing HOI binders.
✓Run #3The email to Elena clearly identifies delay reasons including orphaned files (terminated processors), lock expirations, processing backlog, and missing documentation. For example, it describes 'files stall' due to Andre Washington and Rachel Kim being terminated and still assigned as processors.
✓Run #4The email to Elena explicitly references multiple delay reasons including lock expiration ('Lock expired...'), processing backlog, internal routing errors, compliance add-on delays adding ~1 week per file, Carlos Rivera file quality issues, and appraisal delays.
✓Run #5The email to Elena clearly explains multiple delay reasons: departed staff whose pipelines were never reassigned, Sofia Reyes's overload and missed escalations, expired rate locks, and CTC files not being closed.
✓Run #6The email to Elena includes a detailed 'CONTRIBUTING FACTORS' section referencing terminated staff carrying files (root cause #1), Denise's enhanced compliance review adding ~1 week per file, systemic condition tracking gaps, and lock expirations. Multiple delay reasons are clearly identified.

5 The email content (visible in send_email parameters) sent to [elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com](mailto:elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com) identifies Sofia Reyes or her workload as a contributing factor to the pipeline delays.
outcome
 ✗✓✗✓✓✗ 50%
✗Run #1The email to Elena does mention Sofia Reyes in the processor workload section, but characterizes her as having only ~6 files — the lightest load listed. The email does not identify Sofia's workload as a contributing factor to pipeline delays. The CRM data (which the agent never queried) would have revealed Sofia's actual 34-file caseload and panicked call about being buried, but the agent missed this.
✓Run #2The email to Elena mentions 'Sofia currently has 6+ active files visible in the top pipeline' plus the compliance review burden, directly identifying Sofia Reyes's workload as a contributing factor.
✗Run #3The email content sent to Elena does not mention Sofia Reyes's workload as a contributing factor to the delays. Sofia is only mentioned in the Slack post ('Darnell and Sofia -- expect some files to be redistributed'), not in the Elena email. The agent never queried CRM engagements to find Sofia's 34-file caseload detail.
✓Run #4The email to Elena mentions 'Sofia' multiple times: as the processor on LN-2026-00613, and 'You, Sofia, and Darnell all flagged files stuck on Denise's enhanced review holds' as a root cause of production bottlenecks. Sofia is identified as a contributing factor to delays.
✓Run #5The email to Elena has an entire section titled 'ROOT CAUSE #2 — SOFIA REYES OVERLOAD' that details her missed escalations, specific files on her desk, and the fact that Robert already pulled her aside.
✗Run #6The email to Elena does mention 'Sofia's pipeline has been flagged by Robert' in the Contributing Factors section, but it does not identify Sofia's overloaded workload (e.g., 34 active files, being buried) as a contributing factor to the pipeline delays. The reference to Sofia is about Robert flagging her pipeline, not about her being overburdened.
